what_it_does: An IIFE that registers three additional universal-component KIND Type declarations into window.CV_REGISTRY: a 'panel.composition-menu' panel, a 'kind.graph' graph generator pointing at the DiagramSolver engine, and a 'kind.slide-system' pointing at the Slide engine. Each def carries id/name/kind/layer/family/classification/icon/description plus valueSlots and sockets, and they are registered via R.registerMany with provenance 'built-in'.
observations: Guards on window.CV_REGISTRY existing; logs an error and returns if missing.
composition-menu declares a socket 'items' that accepts ['glyphic','atom','control'] with multiple:true and onPick:'insert' — the headline demo of declarative socket acceptance.
composition-menu also has an event-socket 'onItemClick' (kind:'event', event:'click') accepting ['action','panel','surface'].
kind.graph declares runtime { kind:'engine', key:'DiagramSolver' } and sockets for nodes (glyphic/atom/block) and edges (relationship, optional).
kind.slide-system declares runtime { kind:'engine', key:'Slide' } and a valueSlot 'archetype' as a ref querying CV_REGISTRY.query({family:'deck-slide'}).
valueSlots use varied kinds: text, enum (with values+default), an axis-based 'density' (axis:'space', groups:['inner']), ref.
Comments state load order: after types-core.js + glyphic-type.js + components-type.js.
registerMany is called with { silent: true }.
standouts: The composition-menu socket accepting 'glyphic' classification is explicitly framed as proof that the grammar is universal — acceptance is declarative so candidatesForSocket resolves matching libraries with no bespoke wiring.
Graph and slide kinds are declared as thin Type pointers at pre-existing engines (DiagramSolver, Slide) rather than reimplementing them, making every kind registry-reachable through one registry.
conventions: Follows the registry-as-single-source pattern: extend by registering a Type, never forking a parallel list.
IIFE with 'use strict' and a hard guard that errors loudly if the registry is absent.
Stamps provenance:'built-in' on every def via Object.assign.
Uses 'means' annotations on slots/sockets to document intent inline.
notes: This file widens the universal-component grammar to four kinds by registering a composition-menu panel plus thin Type pointers at the existing graph/slide engines.
questions: Are 'glyphic-type.js' and 'components-type.js' (named as required predecessors) actually loaded before this file in the runtime order?
Does CV_REGISTRY.candidatesForSocket exist and behave as the comments claim, or is that asserted only here?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header comment says it adds the missing composition-menu and registers thin declarations pointing at existing graph/slide engines so every kind is registry-reachable, and the code does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Declarative socket acceptance over bespoke wiring
Universal component grammar (parts/slots/sockets) covering the whole interface
intention_signals: Trying to demonstrate that one registry with a uniform parts/slots/sockets grammar can express every interface kind, including ones backed by separate engines, by pointing at them declaratively rather than special-casing.
novelty: The event-socket concept (a socket with kind:'event' / event:'click' that accepts actions/panels/surfaces) and the explicit framing of socket acceptance as the proof of grammar universality.
contribution: Contributes the composition-menu panel kind and the declarative pattern that wires graph and slide engines into the unified kind registry via thin Type pointers with engine runtime keys.
summary_for_embedding: A CV_REGISTRY registration module declaring three universal-component kinds — a composition-menu panel with glyphic-accepting sockets, plus graph and slide kinds that point at the DiagramSolver and Slide engines.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the universal component grammar covers the whole interface by registering the missing menu panel and making graph/slide kinds reachable through one registry via declarative socket acceptance.
